learning_outcomes.description: Explain the essential biological basis governing normal processes in the body (physiology).
Describe the basis of pharmacology and the principles of treatment of various body system disorders.
Explain how pharmacological principles and drug treatments apply to dentistry.
Describe how physiological and pharmacological experiments are conducted in the laboratory.
Integrate the concepts of physiology and pharmacology to a body system, in health or disease.

assessments.due_date:
P01 and P02 - Quiz component due 27/03/2026 1:00 pm

P01 - Report component due 1/05/2026 12:30 pm

P02 - Report component due 1/05/2026 4:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
This practical assessment encompasses the knowledge gained and results obtained from experiments performed as part of the organ bath prac module (3 practical classes total), and is worth 20% of course assessment. 6% of this will be a quiz at the end of Virtual Ileum (1st prac class) and the remaining 14% will be a combined lab report for the 2 wet lab classes.

This task has been designed to refine students' understanding of pharmacodynamics and exemplify the properties and behaviour of drug agonists and antagonists that are relevant to the dentistry profession. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Details of the assessment format and marking criteria will be provided through the course Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will be in-class during/end of the practical.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The end of semester exam will be based on content covered in all of the lecture modules and overlapping practical content. The exam will be for 90 marks total, with a combination of different question types, covering each module of the course giving a total of 90 marks for the 90 min exam. The allocation of marks to each module will be roughly based on the number of lectures in that module.  

The exam will be a closed book, online exam undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam and format will be provided on Blackboard.

Have your UQ student ID card available for all of your exams.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Prac Module 1: Organ Baths [Borges]

Organ Baths practicals (total 3 classes) run in Weeks 5, 8 and 9 respectively. See your personal timetable for exact days and times.

In Week 5, all students attend Virtual Ileum prac in the same session .

In Weeks 8 and 9, students will attend either P1 (10am-12.30pm) OR P02 (1.30-4pm) for their wet labs, based on individual sign-on.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 5: Respiratory Physiol & Pharmacol [Suen and Kaminskas]

Module 5 deals with Respiratory Physiology & Pharmacology and takes all 3 lectures of Week 9. Dr Jacky Suen will give the 1st of the 3 lectures on respiratory physiology (important also for the spirometry practical later) and A/Prof. Lisa Kaminskas will give the remaining 2 lectures on respiratory pharmacology for the module.

There will be no lectures in Week 10.

There will be no lectures in Week 10.
